accountingError.body: Could not load the data. Check your connection and try again.

bankReconciliation.title: AI reconciliation
bankReconciliation.subtitle: Upload your bank statement and match it against what Avoqado deposited
bankReconciliation.dropTitle: Drag your bank statement (CSV) or click to upload
bankReconciliation.dropHint: We support the CSV your bank exports (BBVA, Banorte, Santander, HSBC…)
bankReconciliation.uploadError: Could not process the file. Make sure it's a CSV with date and amount.
bankReconciliation.empty: You haven't uploaded any statement yet.
bankReconciliation.nothingToConfirm: Nothing to confirm in this statement.
bankReconciliation.disclosureMatch: We reconcile against what Avoqado deposited (electronic payments per day) — Avoqado knows what it paid you, it doesn't guess.
bankReconciliation.disclosureAi: For now we accept CSV. AI PDF reading and fuzzy matching are coming soon.

incomeStatement.title: How much did I make?
incomeStatement.subtitle: Revenue statement for the period
incomeStatement.zeroBody: There are no collected sales in the selected dates. Try widening the date range.
incomeStatement.grossSalesTip: Total billed in the period, before refunds (tax included).
incomeStatement.netRevenueTip: Gross sales minus refunds (what actually came in).
incomeStatement.taxableBaseTip: Amount without VAT, on which the {{pct}}% is calculated.
incomeStatement.tipsTip: They belong to staff; shown for reference only.
incomeStatement.error: Could not load the revenue statement.
incomeStatement.disclosureRevenue: This board shows revenue, not profit: it does not yet subtract cost of goods or expenses.
incomeStatement.disclosureIva: VAT assumes {{pct}}% (general rate); it does not reflect 0% or exempt products.
incomeStatement.disclosureTips: Tips are shown for reference only and are not counted as income.

businessSummary.title: Business summary
businessSummary.subtitle: How you did this period
businessSummary.zeroBody: There are no collected sales in the selected dates. Try widening the date range.
businessSummary.netRevenueTip: What actually came in: sales minus refunds (VAT included).
businessSummary.invoicedPctTip: Share of net revenue already backed by a CFDI stamped in the period.
businessSummary.feesTip: Payment processing fees charged during the period.
businessSummary.netAfterFeesTip: Net revenue minus processing fees. Not profit: it does not yet subtract cost of goods or expenses.
businessSummary.error: Could not load the business summary.
businessSummary.disclosureRevenue: Management summary (Layer A): runs over your payments and CFDIs — not fiscal accounting.
businessSummary.disclosureInvoiced: “Invoiced” is an approximation from the period's stamped CFDIs; it may not match each sale to the cent.
businessSummary.disclosureResult: “Revenue − fees” is not profit: it still excludes cost of goods, payroll and other expenses.

bankAndCash.title: Banks & cash
bankAndCash.subtitle: Your money accounts this period
bankAndCash.cashTotalTip: Total collected in cash: stays in your drawer, never hits the bank.
bankAndCash.electronicTotalTip: Total collected by card, transfer, wallet, etc. (before fees).
bankAndCash.netToBankTip: What the bank should deposit you: electronic minus processing fees.
bankAndCash.disclosureCash: “Cash” is collected cash; it never hits the bank and is not reconciled against your statement.
bankAndCash.disclosureBank: “Bank” groups electronic collections; net to bank subtracts processing fees.

chartOfAccounts.title: Chart of accounts
chartOfAccounts.subtitle: Your ledger accounts with the SAT grouping code
chartOfAccounts.error: Could not load the chart of accounts.
chartOfAccounts.needsFiscalSetupBody: The chart of accounts is per RFC. This venue has no RFC / fiscal issuer configured yet. Set up invoicing (CFDI) and come back.
chartOfAccounts.emptyTitle: You don't have a chart of accounts yet
chartOfAccounts.emptyBody: Generate a base chart already mapped to the SAT grouping code and adapted to your industry. You can adjust it afterward.
chartOfAccounts.emptyNoPermission: Ask an administrator to generate the base chart.

modal.title: Add account
modal.name: Account name
modal.natureHint: Nature (debit/credit) is assigned automatically based on the account type.

chartOfAccounts.disclosureSat: Base chart verified against the SAT grouping code (c_CuentasSAT / Annex 24). You can edit it and add accounts.
chartOfAccounts.disclosureScope: The chart is per taxpayer (RFC), not per venue: all venues under the same RFC share one chart.

toast.seedError: Could not generate the base chart

accountMapping.title: Accounting setup
accountMapping.subtitle: Which account each movement posts to (the system dictates the entries)
accountMapping.needsCatalogTitle: Generate your chart of accounts first
accountMapping.needsCatalogBody: Accounting setup maps each movement to an account in the chart. Generate the chart of accounts and come back here.
accountMapping.emptyTitle: You haven't set up the mapping yet
accountMapping.emptyBody: Generate the default mapping (verified per industry), then adjust it: each movement gets assigned to its account.
accountMapping.error: Could not load the accounting setup.
accountMapping.disclosureEngine: This map is what the journal engine will use to post each entry automatically, without you picking an account each time.
accountMapping.disclosureTaxes: VAT and withholdings are configured separately, in the Taxes section (VAT · DIOT).

periodLock.title: Period close
periodLock.subtitle: Close a month so no journal entries can be posted or edited inside it. Protects what you already filed with the SAT.
periodLock.closeConfirmBody: Once closed, no one can post or edit journal entries within that month. You can reopen it if you need to make corrections.
periodLock.reopenConfirmBody: Journal entries can be posted and edited in that month again. Note: the period may already have been filed with the SAT.
periodLock.modalLocked: Period {{period}} is closed. Reopen it from "Period close" to post in it.

toast.error: Could not update the period lock

journal.title: Journal · Entries
journal.subtitle: Your double-entry postings (each one balances: debits = credits)
journal.emptyTitle: You don't have any entries yet
journal.emptyBody: Record your first manual entry. Each entry must balance: total debits equal total credits.
journal.disclosureBalance: Every entry balances by design: total debits always equal total credits (double-entry).
journal.disclosureAuto: Use "Generate entries" to have the system create the postings on its own, from your payments, using your accounting setup. It is idempotent: re-generating never duplicates.

modal.title: New manual entry
modal.hint: Each line carries a debit OR a credit. The entry saves only when total debits equal total credits.

toast.generateMapping: Some accounts are unmapped in Accounting setup

trialBalance.title: Trial balance
trialBalance.subtitle: Balances and movements per account — the proof your books balance
trialBalance.balancedOk: The trial balance is balanced: debits = credits and debit balance = credit balance ✓
trialBalance.unbalanced: The trial balance does NOT balance — check the period's entries
trialBalance.emptyBody: There are no entries recorded for the selected period. Record entries in the journal.
trialBalance.error: Could not load the trial balance.
trialBalance.disclosureSource: Computed on the fly from the journal entries; nothing is captured separately.
trialBalance.disclosureSign: Balances in blue are credit; normal ones are debit. The closing column shows debit / credit.

accountingReports.title: Accounting reports
accountingReports.subtitle: Income statement and balance sheet, derived from your journal entries
accountingReports.equationOk: The balance sheet balances: Assets = Liabilities + Equity ✓
accountingReports.equationBad: The balance sheet does NOT balance — check the entries
accountingReports.emptyBody: There are no journal entries in the selected period's fiscal year. Record entries in the journal.
accountingReports.error: Could not load the accounting reports.
accountingReports.disclosureSource: Computed on the fly from the journal entries; the income statement accumulates from the start of the fiscal year.
accountingReports.disclosureEquation: The balance sheet balances by design: the period result is added to equity and Assets = Liabilities + Equity.

cashBasisVat.title: Cash-basis VAT · DIOT
cashBasisVat.subtitle: The VAT you owe on what you collected this month (per RFC, sums all your venues)
cashBasisVat.preliminarBanner: PRELIMINARY: this already subtracts your creditable VAT from PAID expenses (CFDI inbox). It still assumes a 16% rate and excludes VAT clients may have withheld on your sales. Do not use as a final payment without your accountant.
cashBasisVat.zeroBody: No sales collected this period. REMEMBER: even with no activity, you usually still have to file a zero VAT return.
cashBasisVat.multiOrg: This RFC operates venues across more than one Avoqado organization; the calculation sums them all (it's per taxpayer).
cashBasisVat.ivaAcreditableHint: VAT from your supplier CFDIs PAID this month (cash-basis). Reduces the VAT you owe.
cashBasisVat.ivaAPagarHint: Sales side only; it will be lower once your expense VAT is subtracted.
cashBasisVat.cfdiContrasteHint: Informational: VAT on the invoices stamped this month. Not the base: accrued VAT comes from what was COLLECTED, not invoiced.
cashBasisVat.diotBody: The DIOT lists the VAT you paid to each supplier (expense side). Requires recording your received CFDIs (coming soon, in the CFDI inbox).
cashBasisVat.error: Could not load the cash-basis VAT summary.
cashBasisVat.disclosureCashBasis: VAT accrues on a CASH-FLOW basis: on what you actually collected in the month, not on what you invoiced.
cashBasisVat.disclosureNotInvoiced: The 16% rate is a general assumption; venues with 0%, 8% (border) or exempt products will see VAT overstated.
cashBasisVat.disclosureSaldoFavor: Carrying forward or applying a credit balance is your and your accountant's decision; Avoqado does not apply it automatically.
cashBasisVat.ivaRetenidoTercerosHint: The VAT YOU withheld from suppliers; remitted SEPARATELY, it does not reduce the VAT you owe.
cashBasisVat.retencionesHint: VAT a client may have withheld from you on sales (not captured yet).
cashBasisVat.diotEmpty: No paid expenses this period. Register your supplier CFDIs in the inbox and mark them paid.
cashBasisVat.diotMismatch: ⚠ DIOT VAT does not match the cash-basis VAT; check the per-rate breakdown.

payLaterAging.title: Accounts Receivable
payLaterAging.subtitle: "Pay Later" orders grouped by aging
payLaterAging.detailSubtitle: Pending payment, sorted from most to least overdue
payLaterAging.searchPlaceholder: Search by order, customer or table...

empty.title: No accounts receivable
empty.subtitle: All "Pay Later" orders are settled. Well done!

error.title: Error
error.load: Failed to load the aging report: {{message}}

settle.successDesc: Order {{order}} has been marked as paid
settle.notesHelp: Add details about how the payment was received

upsell.title: Unlock full reporting with Pro
upsell.history: View sales from any day or date range
upsell.periods: Compare hourly, daily, weekly and monthly periods
upsell.breakdowns: Detailed breakdowns by terminal, merchant and payment type

export.title: Export

mode.label: What do you want to export?

sections.label: Sections to include
sections.cardTypesDisabledHint: Remove the payment-method filter to include card-type detail.

format.label: Format
format.csvHint: Lightweight. Works with Excel and Google Sheets.

export.tooLargeHint: Narrow the range with filters or use CSV/Excel.

premium.title: Detailed export requires Premium
premium.body: Export one row per transaction with every column in CSV, Excel or PDF.

tooltips.grossSales: Total sales amount before discounts and refunds are applied
tooltips.items: All item sales, excluding taxes, discounts, comps, and refunds
tooltips.serviceCosts: Service costs including integrated tips, surcharges, and other fees
tooltips.discounts: Discounts applied to orders, including promotions and coupons
tooltips.refunds: Refunds processed during the selected period
tooltips.netSales: Gross sales minus discounts and refunds (Gross sales - Discounts - Refunds)
tooltips.deferredSales: Total amount charged to customers for orders that are still open or pending payment
tooltips.taxes: Taxes collected on sales during the period
tooltips.tips: Tips received by staff during the period
tooltips.platformFees: Fees charged by Avoqado for payment processing and platform usage
tooltips.staffCommissions: Commissions paid to employees based on venue commission configurations
tooltips.totalSales: Net sales (taxes are already included in prices)
tooltips.totalCollected: Money received: net sales + tips - platform fees
tooltips.totalCollectedSettlement: Deposit timing varies by payment method: debit and credit cards settle in {{debitDays}} business day(s), while Amex and international cards take {{amexDays}} business day(s).
tooltips.netProfit: True profit after all costs: net sales - platform fees - staff commissions

reportType.label: Report type
reportType.description: View the report in hourly, daily, weekly or monthly intervals.
reportType.longDescription: Changing the report type will change how your data is displayed. Summary reports will show single total figures, while others may show changes by day, for example.

options.summaryDesc: Groups the same metrics from different days as total values, without segmenting them chronologically
options.hourlySumDesc: Groups metrics from different days by hour (e.g., all sales recorded between 9:00 and 9:59, between 10:00 and 10:59, etc.)
options.dailySumDesc: Groups metrics from different days by day of the week (e.g., all sales recorded on Tuesdays)
options.hoursDesc: Shows data for each hour of the selected period
options.daysDesc: Shows data for each day of the selected period
options.weeksDesc: Shows data for each week of the selected period
options.monthsDesc: Shows data for each month of the selected period

viewType.label: View
viewType.description: Change how your information is displayed
viewType.longDescription: Select how you want to visualize your report data.

terminal.label: Terminals
terminal.longDescription: Select which terminals to include in the report.

merchant.label: Merchant
merchant.longDescription: Select a specific merchant account to view only sales processed through it. Useful when you have multiple affiliations or processors.

groupBy.label: Group metrics by
groupBy.description: Group metrics in the data table
groupBy.longDescription: Segment the report data by different criteria.

options.originDesc: Group by order type (dine-in, takeout, delivery)
options.channelDesc: Group by sales channel (TPV, QR, Web, App)
options.paymentMethodDesc: Group by payment type (cash, card, digital)
options.staffDesc: Group by cashier or server who processed the sale

filterBy.label: Filter by
filterBy.description: Restrict the report to a payment method or card type
filterBy.longDescription: Filter to show only payments matching the chosen method or card type. Order-level metrics (gross sales, taxes, discounts) are hidden under filter because they cannot be attributed to a single payment method.
filterBy.filteredMessage: Clear the filter to see the full distribution

options.cardDesc: Credit or debit card payments only
options.qrLegacyDesc: Payments from the previous QR system

metrics.label: Metrics
metrics.description: Choose which metrics to show in the report
metrics.longDescription: Selected metrics will be shown in the report and exports.
metrics.chartHint: The selected metric will be shown in the report. If you select the chart icon, it will modify the report visualization.

options.grossSalesDesc: Item sales, including modifiers and service costs
options.itemsDesc: All item sales, excluding taxes, discounts, comps, and refunds
options.serviceCostsDesc: Service costs including integrated tips, surcharges, and other fees
options.discountsDesc: Deductions applied to your sales for promotions, coupons, or comps
options.refundsDesc: All itemized refunds, excluding partial refunds
options.netSalesDesc: Gross sales minus refunds, discounts, and comps
options.deferredSalesDesc: Total charged for orders that are still open or pending payment
options.taxesDesc: Tax amount applied at the time of sale
options.tipsDesc: Tips received by staff during the period
options.platformFeesDesc: Fees charged by Avoqado for payment processing
options.staffCommissionsDesc: Commissions paid to employees based on venue configurations
options.totalCollectedDesc: Net sales plus taxes, tips, and other charges
options.netProfitDesc: True profit after all costs (including staff commissions)

statement.title: Period statement

timeline.title: When it lands
timeline.unprojected: No estimated date: {{amount}} (accounts without a settlement rule)
timeline.note: Estimated from each merchant's settlement rules (business days + holidays). Real confirmation will arrive once we connect your bank. Cash isn't shown here — it's already in your hand.

statement.filteredNote: The statement is shown without payment-method filters.

settlementWeek.title: When it lands (week)
settlementWeek.note: How much lands in your bank each day, by settlement date. Estimated from each merchant's rules (business days + holidays). Cash isn't shown here — it's already in your hand.

salesByItem.title: Sales by Item
salesByItem.subtitle: Item sales breakdown for the period
salesByItem.noData: No sales data for this period

hoursFilter.label: Daily report hours
hoursFilter.description: Filter data by hours of the day
hoursFilter.longDescription: Choose whether to include all hours of the day or specify a custom time range.

reportType.label: Report type
reportType.description: View the report in hourly, daily, weekly or monthly intervals.

groupBy.label: Show by
groupBy.longDescription: Choose how to segment items in the report. This affects how data is organized in the table.

salesByCategory.title: Sales by Category
salesByCategory.subtitle: Category sales breakdown for the period
salesByCategory.noData: No sales data for this period

hoursFilter.label: Daily report hours
hoursFilter.description: Filter data by hours of the day
hoursFilter.longDescription: Choose whether to include all hours of the day or specify a custom time range.

expenses.title: CFDI Inbox · Expenses
expenses.subtitle: The CFDIs your suppliers issue you. They enable your creditable VAT, the DIOT and real costs (by tax ID).
expenses.emptyTitle: You haven't registered expenses this period
expenses.emptyBody: Register the CFDIs your suppliers issue you to deduct, credit VAT and build your DIOT.
expenses.disclosureCashBasis: An expense's VAT becomes creditable when you PAY it (cash-basis), not when you receive it.
expenses.disclosurePost: Generate the entries so the expense flows into your journal, trial balance and reports.

toast.generateNone: No new expenses to post ({{already}} already done)
toast.markPaidError: Could not mark the expense as paid

modal.title: Add expense
modal.name: Name / legal name
modal.paid: I've already paid it (VAT credited this period)
modal.hint: The total is computed: subtotal − discount + VAT + excise − withholdings. It must match your CFDI.

expenses.markPaidBody: Record the payment to {{name}}. VAT becomes creditable in the month of the payment date.

electronicAccounting.title: Electronic accounting (SAT · Anexo 24)
electronicAccounting.body: Download your chart of accounts and trial balance in the SAT 1.3 XML format, ready for your accountant to seal with the e.firma and submit.
electronicAccounting.disclaimer: Unsealed XML. Validate and seal it with your accountant (e.firma) before submitting to the SAT.
electronicAccounting.empty: No data to generate the XML for this period.

accountsPayable.title: Accounts payable
accountsPayable.subtitle: Who you owe and since when (supplier balance aging).
accountsPayable.empty: You have no outstanding supplier balances as of this date.
accountsPayable.disclosure: From Inbox expenses with a pending balance (total − paid). Aging is counted from the CFDI issue date.

fiscalReadiness.title: Tax readiness
fiscalReadiness.subtitle: What this venue still needs to start running fiscal accounting.
fiscalReadiness.disclosure: This check only reviews your setup; it sends nothing to the SAT. Green = ready, amber = review, red = missing.

isr.title: Income tax · Provisional payment
isr.subtitle: Estimate of your monthly income tax (individual, by tax ID).
isr.preliminarBanner: ESTIMATE: it excludes prior-year losses and profit-sharing. VAT is computed by real rate (16%/8%/0%) and only falls back to 16% where no rate applies. The general-regime tariff is the 2024/2025 one (in force in 2026 unless newly published). Do not use as a final payment without your accountant.
isr.zeroBody: No sales collected this period: no income tax accrues. Remember to file even if it is zero.
isr.excedeTope: Your cumulative income exceeds $3,500,000/year: you can no longer be in RESICO; switch to the general regime.
isr.ingresosMesHint: What you actually collected this month, excluding VAT (all your venues under the tax ID).
isr.deduccionesHint: Your deductible expenses PAID this year (CFDI inbox).
isr.costoVentasHint: Cost of inventory consumed during the year (recipes and products). Deductible under the general regime.
isr.pagosPreviosHint: Estimated income tax from earlier months of the year.
isr.retencionesIsrHint: ISR your corporate clients withheld (captured below). Reduces your ISR due.
isr.disclosureEstimate: This is an estimate: your accountant computes and files the final income tax.
isr.disclosureRegime: RESICO applies a low rate (1%–2.5%) on collections, with no deductions. The general regime applies the progressive tariff on your profit (income − deductions).
isr.disclosureTariff: It excludes VAT, social security, payroll income-tax withholding and state payroll tax; those are computed separately.

retencion.title: Sales withholding for the period
retencion.description: If your clients are companies (personas morales), they withheld ISR and IVA when paying you. Capture it here so your ISR and IVA due aren't overstated. Leave zero or blank if nothing was withheld.
retencion.notePlaceholder: E.g. withholding from Client Inc. per CFDI…
retencion.notCaptured: Not captured yet for this period.

nomina.title: Payroll
nomina.subtitle: Your employees and the period payroll run (estimate, by tax ID).
nomina.preliminarBanner: ESTIMATE: income tax uses the art-96 tariff minus the employment subsidy (2026) and the employee IMSS is estimated at 2.375% of salary. The precise SBC calc and the payroll CFDI stamping are missing. Do not use as a final pay stub without your payroll specialist.
nomina.emptyBody: Add your employees to compute their income tax, social security and net pay, and post the payroll entry.
nomina.disclosureEstimate: Estimated calc: income tax is art-96 tariff − subsidy; employee IMSS ~2.375% of SBC. The final figure is your payroll specialist’s.
nomina.disclosurePoliza: Running payroll posts the entry (salaries · income tax withheld · IMSS · salaries payable) into your journal.

toast.runExists: A run already exists for this period
toast.runMapping: Missing payroll accounts in Accounting setup
toast.stampNeedsCsd: The issuer has no active digital seal (CSD); upload it in Invoicing
toast.stampErrors: Stamped {{stamped}}, with {{errors}} error(s) — check the data

modal.title: New employee
modal.hint: The monthly gross salary is the base for the income tax and IMSS calc. You can enter the exact SBC later.
